ucs.title: [LLM] APT1 (Comment Crew / Unit 61398) canned domain-recon batch chain
ucs.description: Hunts the signature APT1 hands-on-keyboard recon sequence — a single cmd.exe / batch parent firing net.exe, ipconfig, tasklist, netstat and systeminfo in quick succession with the distinctive 'net group "Domain Admins" /domain' enumeration documented in Mandiant's APT1 report. Comment Crew operators repeatedly dropped near-identical recon batch files on initial footholds before pivoting with PsExec / RDP.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents dc(Processes.process_name) as recon_bin_dc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" Processes.user!="SYSTEM" Processes.user!="LOCAL SERVICE" Processes.user!="NETWORK SERVICE" (Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","nbtstat.exe","quser.exe","net view","hostname.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_id _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where recon_bin_dc>=5 AND match(cmdlines, "(?i)(domain admins|/domain|net\s+group|net\s+user)") | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let WindowMin = 10m;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","nbtstat.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where FileName in~ (ReconBins)
| extend NetSubcmd = iif(FileName =~ "net.exe" or FileName =~ "net1.exe",
                         tostring(extract(@"(?i)net1?\.exe\s+(\w+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)),
                         "")
| summarize ReconBinCount = dcount(FileName),
            EventCount    = count(),
            FirstSeen     = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen      = max(Timestamp),
            CmdLines      = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 50),
            NetSubcmds    = make_set(NetSubcmd, 20),
            Parents       = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
            ParentCmds    = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 10),
            DurationSec   = datetime_diff('second', max(Timestamp), min(Timestamp))
            by DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessId, bin(Timestamp, WindowMin)
| where ReconBinCount >= 5                                  // canned chain hits ≥5 distinct recon bins
| where CmdLines has_any ("Domain Admins","domain admins","/domain","net group","net user")
| where DurationSec <= 300                                  // all within 5 min — script, not human
| where Parents has_any ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let WindowMin = 10m;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","nbtstat.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where AccountType == "User"
| extend ImageName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\\")[-1]))
| where ImageName in (ReconBins)
| extend NetSubcmd = iif(ImageName in ("net.exe","net1.exe"),
                         tostring(extract(@"(?i)net1?\.exe\s+(\w+)", 1, CommandLine)),
                         "")
| summarize ReconBinCount = dcount(ImageName),
            EventCount   = count(),
            FirstSeen    = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen     = max(TimeGenerated),
            CmdLines     = make_set(CommandLine, 50),
            NetSubcmds   = make_set(NetSubcmd, 20),
            Parents      = make_set(ParentProcessName, 10),
            DurationSec  = datetime_diff('second', max(TimeGenerated), min(TimeGenerated))
            by Computer, Account, SubjectLogonId, bin(TimeGenerated, WindowMin)
| where ReconBinCount >= 5
| where CmdLines has_any ("Domain Admins","domain admins","/domain","net group","net user")
| where DurationSec <= 300
| where Parents has_any ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Mandiant's M-Trends APT1 report explicitly catalogues the canned recon batch files Unit 61398 ran on every fresh foothold — the same five-to-eight `net`/`ipconfig`/`tasklist` calls in the same order, almost always including `net group "Domain Admins" /domain`. Detecting one recon binary is dashboard-grade noise; detecting ≥5 distinct recon binaries from the same parent inside 5 minutes that *also*

ucs.title: [LLM] APT1 RAR staging for exfiltration — password-protected, multi-volume archive in user-writeable path
ucs.description: Hunts APT1's documented data-staging tradecraft: a renamed/dropped rar.exe (often placed in C:\RECYCLER\, %TEMP%, or a user profile path) invoked with the distinctive `-hp<password> -v<size>m -m5` flag combination to produce password-protected split archives prior to FTP/HTTP exfil. Mandiant attributed this exact RAR command shape to multiple Comment Crew intrusions and it remains a high-fidelity Unit 61398 signature.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" (Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","winrar.exe","rar32.exe","rar64.exe") OR match(Processes.process, "(?i)\s+a\s+-(hp|hp\S+|v\d+|m5)\s+")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(cmdline, "(?i)\s+-hp\S+") AND match(cmdline, "(?i)\s+-(v\d+[kmg]?|m5|m4|r|inul)\b") AND NOT match(process_path, "(?i)\\\\Program Files( \\(x86\\))?\\\\WinRAR\\\\") | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let RarBins = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","rar32.exe","rar64.exe"]);
let TrustedRarPaths = dynamic([@"c:\program files\winrar\", @"c:\program files (x86)\winrar\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (RarBins)
   or (ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s+a\s+-hp\S+" 
       and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-v","-m5","-r ","-inul"))
| extend LowerPath = tolower(FolderPath)
| extend HasPwdFlag    = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-hp\S+",
         HasVolumeFlag = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-v\d+[kmgKMG]?\b",
         HasArchiveOp  = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s+(a|m|u)\s+",
         HasMaxCompr   = ProcessCommandLine has "-m5",
         HasRecurse    = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-r(\s|$)",
         RenamedRar    = FileName !in~ (RarBins) and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s+a\s+-hp\S+"
| where HasPwdFlag and HasArchiveOp and (HasVolumeFlag or HasMaxCompr or HasRecurse or RenamedRar)
| where not(LowerPath has_any (TrustedRarPaths))               // exclude vendor-installed WinRAR
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentSHA   = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          RenamedRar, HasPwdFlag, HasVolumeFlag, HasMaxCompr
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let RarBins = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","rar32.exe","rar64.exe"]);
let TrustedRarPaths = dynamic([@"c:\program files\winrar\", @"c:\program files (x86)\winrar\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where AccountType == "User"
| extend ImageName  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\\")[-1])),
         LowerPath  = tolower(NewProcessName)
| where ImageName in (RarBins)
   or (CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s+a\s+-hp\S+" 
       and CommandLine has_any ("-v","-m5","-r ","-inul"))
| extend HasPwdFlag    = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-hp\S+",
         HasVolumeFlag = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-v\d+[kmgKMG]?\b",
         HasArchiveOp  = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s+(a|m|u)\s+",
         HasMaxCompr   = CommandLine has "-m5",
         HasRecurse    = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-r(\s|$)",
         RenamedRar    = ImageName !in (RarBins) and CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s+a\s+-hp\S+"
| where HasPwdFlag and HasArchiveOp and (HasVolumeFlag or HasMaxCompr or HasRecurse or RenamedRar)
| where not(LowerPath has_any (TrustedRarPaths))
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName,
          RenamedRar, HasPwdFlag, HasVolumeFlag, HasMaxCompr, HasRecurse
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT1 Comment Crew RAR Staging Pattern
id: 8f2c7a14-apt1-rar-stage-c4e9-b3d1f7062894
status: experimental
description: Detects the password-protected, multi-volume RAR archive command line documented as APT1 / Unit 61398 staging tradecraft (rar a -hp<pw> -v<size>m -m5 ...). Catches both legit-named rar.exe outside Program Files and renamed RAR binaries.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0006/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/sites/default/files/2021-09/mandiant-apt1-report.pdf
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
  - attack.exfiltration
  - attack.collection
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0006
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_rar_name:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rar.exe'
      - '\winrar.exe'
      - '\rar32.exe'
      - '\rar64.exe'
  selection_rar_cli:
    CommandLine|re|i: '\s+a\s+-hp\S+'
  selection_signature_flags:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -hp'
    CommandLine|re|i:
      - '\s-(v\d+[kmg]?|m5|inul|r\s)'
  filter_legit_install:
    Image|contains|all:
      - '\Program Files'
      - '\WinRAR\'
  condition: (selection_rar_name or selection_rar_cli) and selection_signature_flags and not filter_legit_install
falsepositives:
  - Backup administrators using RAR with passworded multi-volume archives outside the default install path (rare; allowlist by host).
level: high
ucs.rationale: The Mandiant APT1 report (Appendix B / case studies) repeatedly cites RAR invocations of the form `rar a -hp<pwd> -v200m -m5 <archive> <target>` against staged collection directories — a highly specific combination of password protection, volume splitting, and max compression that legitimate users almost never run together outside the WinRAR install directory. Pairing the rare flag-combo with path
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
